research_question: Can open-source slide-code and PPT-generation projects teach concrete implementation patterns for Vulca's code-generated editable PPT workflow?

usage_boundary.reason: Open-source repositories can teach APIs, abstractions, verification patterns, and export pipelines. Vulca must still generate original case-specific content, geometry, and PPTX source code.

must_show: what each project is useful for
what concrete code patterns are learnable
how the learning maps to code-generated editable PPT
which Run 2.58 artifact proves current consumption

open_source_reference_records.label: PptxGenJS
open_source_reference_records.license_or_usage_note: Use the public library/API under its repository license; do not copy project demo decks or visual assets as Vulca output.
open_source_reference_records.what_to_learn: How a JavaScript PPTX writer models slides, shapes, text runs, charts, images, tables, masters, layout units, and Office-compatible export.
open_source_reference_records.integration_boundary: Vulca can learn object-model and export patterns, but its generator should continue producing original native PPT modules and trace manifests.
open_source_reference_records.product_relevance: Closest implementation reference for code-generated editable PPT because it targets actual PPTX output rather than static images.

open_source_reference_records.label: Slidev
open_source_reference_records.license_or_usage_note: Learn framework structure and theme/component patterns; do not copy themes, example talks, or creator-specific visual identity.
open_source_reference_records.what_to_learn: How a developer-oriented slide system turns Markdown, Vue components, themes, live code, diagrams, annotations, and export paths into a reusable presentation runtime.
learnable_code_patterns: theme package and layout slot conventions
export-oriented separation between source and render
open_source_reference_records.integration_boundary: Useful for HTML preview/workflow ideas, but Vulca's final product promise is editable PPTX, so Slidev patterns should be translated into PPT-native modules.
open_source_reference_records.product_relevance: Good reference for slide-as-code authoring ergonomics and live preview, weaker reference for native PPT editability.

open_source_reference_records.label: Marp
open_source_reference_records.license_or_usage_note: Learn Markdown-to-slide conversion and CSS theme organization; do not reuse demo themes as product output without an explicit license review and redesign.
open_source_reference_records.what_to_learn: How a Markdown presentation ecosystem handles slide splitting, directives, CSS theming, built-in themes, CLI conversion, and export to HTML/PDF/PPTX.
learnable_code_patterns: Markdown page delimiter and directive grammar
theme CSS as a constrained visual system
small source document to multiple output formats
open_source_reference_records.integration_boundary: Useful for content-source discipline and export mental model; Vulca should not reduce high-design commercial decks to markdown-theme sameness.
open_source_reference_records.product_relevance: Useful baseline for reproducible deck source and theme mechanics; not enough by itself for high-end product-demo composition.

open_source_reference_records.label: reveal.js
open_source_reference_records.license_or_usage_note: Learn runtime, fragments, plugin boundaries, and navigation patterns; do not treat HTML animation as proof of native Keynote/PPT animation.
open_source_reference_records.what_to_learn: How an HTML presentation framework structures decks, fragments, transitions, plugin APIs, keyboard navigation, speaker notes, and theme CSS.
open_source_reference_records.integration_boundary: Useful for HTML viewer and motion proof prototypes, but final Vulca PPTX must explicitly prove native animation/editability when that is claimed.
open_source_reference_records.product_relevance: Strong reference for preview/runtime and demo-video surfaces, weaker reference for editable PPTX generation.

open_source_reference_records.label: SlideCoder
open_source_reference_records.license_or_usage_note: Use as research inspiration only; inspect license and data terms before any direct dependency, and never copy benchmark images or generated examples as Vulca content.
open_source_reference_records.what_to_learn: How layout-aware retrieval-augmented hierarchical slide generation can decompose a design target, retrieve examples, and generate slide code with traceable intermediate decisions.
learnable_code_patterns: RAG example retrieval for slide structure
hierarchical planning from slide to visual objects
open_source_reference_records.integration_boundary: This is the closest research analogy for design-memory to slide-code. Vulca should learn workflow shape, not copy data, layouts, or outputs.
open_source_reference_records.product_relevance: Directly relevant to our data -> design memory -> skill workflow -> code-generated editable PPT thesis.

next_workflow_obligation.action: Convert the five reference records into a Vulca-native code-pattern memory: PPTX object model, source-to-render pipeline, theme/component boundary, motion/runtime boundary, and RAG-to-layout planning.
next_workflow_obligation.must_not_do: Do not add more rectangles or style variations until the generator proves it uses at least one concrete code-pattern memory record per slide role.
